message:
fix(db): configure pool_reset_on_return to prevent SQLAlchemy GC warnings

Add pool_reset_on_return="rollback" to the AsyncEngine configuration to
ensure uncommitted transactions are rolled back when connections are
returned to the pool. This prevents the SQLAlchemy warning:

  "The garbage collector is trying to clean up non-checked-in connection"

which occurs when connections with uncommitted work are garbage collected
instead of being properly closed.

Key changes:
- Add pool_reset_on_return="rollback" to _build_engine() configuration
- Add detailed docstring to get_session() explaining the cleanup strategy

IMPORTANT: We intentionally do NOT call session.rollback() in get_session()
because that would expire all loaded ORM objects, causing DetachedInstanceError
when code tries to access attributes after the session closes. The pool-level
rollback handles uncommitted transactions safely without expiring objects.

This is a defensive configuration change that improves reliability in
multi-agent environments where sessions may not always be cleanly closed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -242,6 +242,7 @@ def convert_datetime(val: bytes | str) -> dt_module.datetime | None:
         max_overflow=max_overflow,
         pool_timeout=30,  # Fail fast with clear error instead of hanging indefinitely
         pool_recycle=3600,  # Recycle connections after 1 hour to prevent stale handles
+        pool_reset_on_return="rollback",  # Ensure uncommitted transactions are rolled back on return
         connect_args=connect_args,
     )
 
@@ -335,6 +336,17 @@ def get_session_factory() -> async_sessionmaker[AsyncSession]:
 
 @asynccontextmanager
 async def get_session() -> AsyncIterator[AsyncSession]:
+    """Provide an async database session with guaranteed cleanup.
+
+    This context manager ensures the session is always closed, even under task
+    cancellation. Uses asyncio.shield() to prevent cancellation from interrupting
+    the close operation.
+
+    Note: We do NOT call session.rollback() here because that would expire all
+    loaded objects, causing DetachedInstanceError when code tries to access
+    attributes after the session closes. The pool_reset_on_return='rollback'
+    setting handles uncommitted transactions at the pool level instead.
+    """
     factory = get_session_factory()
     session = factory()
     try:
